rating.relevance_remarks: Content is on Green IT challenges - relevant to the stated topic. However, appears to be a continuation from previous module (The Agenda And Challenges Ahead) rather than a distinct standalone topic.
rating.coverage_remarks: Content is severely truncated - ends mid-sentence. Only covers 2 challenge areas (energy/carbon, e-waste) before cutting off. Missing coverage of software challenges, supply chain, policy/regulation, skills gap, ROI challenges.
rating.quality_remarks: Well-written with good structure but MCQ is too easy (marked easy) for Standard tier. Should include numerical problems, application-based questions. Flashcard is basic definition.
rating.tier_remarks: Content depth appears introductory rather than thorough. Expected formal academic tone with proofs/frameworks at B.Tech level. MCQ difficulty easy is inappropriate - should be hard. Read.md appears truncated at ~3000 chars.
suggestions: Expand content to cover minimum 200-250 lines as expected
Add harder MCQs: calculate PUE from given data, analyze trade-offs between sustainability and performance
Include more technical depth: specific cooling technologies, carbon footprint calculation methods, green coding practices
Add frameworks for overcoming challenges rather than just listing them
Ensure content is complete and not truncated mid-sentence

rating.quality_remarks: Writing is clear but MCQ difficulty is 'easy' - inappropriate for Sem 4 where hard/application-based questions expected. Flashcard is basic recall. Content abrupt cut-off suggests incomplete material.

Add deeper analysis of how each industrial revolution impacted environmental sustainability
Replace 'easy' MCQ with application-based question linking industrial history to Green IT challenges
Expand content to 200-250 lines with formal academic tone and technical vocabulary
Add numerical/scenario-based MCQs analyzing sustainability trade-offs
Include specific examples of how Industry 4.0 enables green technologies
